<current_datetime>2026-04-03T01:50:55.197Z</current_datetime>

Migrate this .NET Framework code to .NET 8. Provide complete migration guidance with code and pitfalls.

```csharp
public class EventProcessor
{
    private readonly ManualResetEvent _newEvent = new ManualResetEvent(false);
    private readonly Queue<string> _queue = new Queue<string>();
    private Thread _thread;
    private readonly object _lock = new object();

    public void Start()
    {
        _thread = new Thread(RunLoop);
        _thread.Start();
    }

    private void RunLoop()
    {
        while (true)
        {
            _newEvent.WaitOne(); // blocks until signaled
            _newEvent.Reset();

            string item;
            lock (_lock) { item = _queue.Dequeue(); }
            Handle(item);
        }
    }

    public void Enqueue(string item)
    {
        lock (_lock) { _queue.Enqueue(item); }
        _newEvent.Set();
    }

    public void Stop()
    {
        _thread.Interrupt(); // wake from WaitOne
        _thread.Join(5000);
        if (_thread.IsAlive)
            _thread.Abort();
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to migrate a .NET Framework `EventProcessor` class to .NET 8. Let me analyze the code and provide complete migration guidance.

Key issues in this code:
1. `Thread.Abort()` - throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` in .NET 8 (removed since .NET 5)
2. `Thread.Interrupt()` - throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` in .NET 6+
3. `ManualResetEvent` + manual locking + `Queue` - should be replaced with `Channel<T>` or `BlockingCollection<T>`
4. Raw `Thread` usage - should use `Task` with async/await


5. There's also a race condition where multiple items can queue up but only one gets processed per signal, and the immediate reset loses pending signals
6. No cancellation support - I need to add `CancellationToken` throughout for proper cleanup
